semanticCore: Whispering, muttering, low voice, silence, secrecy, or death
quranicRelevance: Whispering to one another in secret (Takhafatoo - 'yatakhafatoona baynahum'), and not lowering voice in prayer ('la tukhafit').

keyDerivations.meaning: To lower the voice, whisper, speak in secret, or mute sound
To whisper to one another, mutter in secret, or converse quietly

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَلَا تَجْهَرْ بِصَلَاتِكَ وَلَا تُخَافِتْ بِهَا وَابْتَغِ بَيْنَ ذَٰلِكَ سَبِيلًا (17:110): And do not recite too loudly in your prayer, nor lower/whisper it too much, but seek a way in between.
discardedNoise: Sudden death (Khafatan) of birds in autumn.
summaryEn: The root denotes sound reduction and whispering, regulating prayer acoustics (avoiding excessive whispering - Khawat) and detailing sinners whispering in terror at resurrection.
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على خفض الصوت والسرار التخافتي (خفت)، ضابطاً مستويات صوت الصلاة المتوسطة (ولا تخافت بها)، ومصوراً هامس المجرمين المذعورين.
rawText: اخفتت 4 She (a camel) brought forth on the day [of the year] in which she was impregnated [or just a year after she was covered]. (K.) تخافتوا 6 They consulted together secretly. (TA.) See also 1. === And تخافت † He feigned, or made a false show of, weakness and stillness. (TA.) خافت 3 , inf. n. مُخَافَتَةٌ: see 1, in four places. === [Hence,] الإِبِلُ تُخَافِتُ المَضْغَ The camels ruminate. (TA.) خفّت 2 [app. It silenced, or killed: said of a smiting with a sword or the like: see فَوَّارٌ]. (TA.) خَافِتٌ A voice becoming still, or silent; or low, or suppressed; as also 🡻خَفِيتٌ. (A.) You say صَوْتٌ خَفِيضٌ 🡻خَفِيتٌ. (TA.) === One whose voice is still, or silent, by reason of his weakness. (Har p. 76.) Applied to a dying man, Ceasing to speak; silent. (S.) === سَحَابٌ خَافِتٌ Clouds in which is no water. (Aboo-Sa'eed, K.) A cloud like these does not move from its place: only that which contains water travels along: that which sends forth a slightly-flashing lightning scarcely ever, or never, does so. (Aboo-Sa'eed.) === زَرْعٌ خَافِتٌ ‡ Dying, or dead, seedproduce: (A:) or seed-produce that has not grown tall: (Msb, * K, TA:) or that has not attained the full height. (TA.) The weak believer is likened, in a trad., to خَافِتُ الزَّرْعِ, (A, TA,) which at one time inclines, and at another time stands straight; accord. to A 'Obeyd, خافت meaning what is fresh, or juicy, and soft, or tender: or, accord. to one reading, to خَافِتَةُ الزَّرْعِ, meaning fresh, or juicy, seed-produce, that is soft, or tender, and weak; the ة being added in خافته as though by زرع were meant سُنْبُلَة. (TA.) خَفَتَ 1 , (S, A, Msb, K,) aor. ﹻ, (Msb,) inf. n. خُفُوتٌ (S, A, K) and خَفْتٌ (Msb, TA) and خُفَاتٌ, (TA,) It (the voice, S, A, Msb) was, or became, still, (S, K,) or silent; (A;) was, or became, low, (Msb,) or soft, or gentle, or slender; and became weak, by reason of vehement hunger. (TA.) === Hence, said of a dying man, He ceased speaking; (S;) he was, or became, silent; (S, A, K;) he spoke not. (A.) === And [hence,] خَفَتَ, (A, TA,) inf. n. خُفُوتٌ. † He died: (A, TA:) and خَفَتَ, inf. n. خُفَاتٌ, ‡ he died suddenly; (AA, S, K, TA;) as also مَاتَ خُفَاتًا. (A.) And the latter, i. e. خَفَتَ inf. n. خُفَاتٌ, accord. to AM, † He was, or became, weak, and abject, or abased. (TA.) === خَفْتٌ also signifies The speaking with a suppressed voice; and so 🡻تَخَافُتٌ, (S, K, TA,) and 🡻مُخَافَتَةٌ. (K, TA.) And you say, خَفَتَ بِصَوْتِهِ, (Msb,) and 🡻خافتبِهِ, (TA,) He lowered his voice; spoke with a low voice. (Msb, TA.) And خَفَتَ بِقِرَآءَتِهِ; (TA;) and 🡻خافتبِهَا, (A, Msb,) inf. n. مُخَافَتَةٌ; (Msb;) and 🡻قَرَأَمُخَافَتَةً; (TA;) He lowered his voice in his reading or reciting; read, or recited, with a low voice: (A, Msb, TA:) or the second of these signifies he read, or recited, indistinctly, not with raised voice. (Lth, TA.) === خَفَتَ, said of seed-produce, † It was, or became, such as is termed خَافِتٌ [explained below]. (Msb.) خَفُوتٌ ‡ A lean, or an emaciated, woman: (Lh, K:) or a woman who is scarcely seen distinctly, by reason of leanness, or emaciation: (TA:) or a woman who is deemed goodly, or beautiful, (K,) whom the eye regards as worthy of notice, (A, TA,) as long as she is alone, not when she is among other women. (A, K, TA.) You say اِمْرَأَةٌ خَفْوتٌ لَفُوتٌ: (Lth, A, TA:) لفوت meaning wont to calumniate, or slander. (A.) But AM says, I have not heard خفوت as an epithet applied to a woman on any authority but that of Lth. (TA.) خَفِيتٌ : see what follows, in two places. خَفْتٌ (S) and 🡻خُفَاتٌ, (A,) applied to speech, (S, A,) Uttered with a low, or suppressed, voice. (S, * A.) [See also خَافِتٌ.] ___ The former is also syn. with خَبْتٌ [A low, or depressed, tract of ground: &c.]. (K. [So accord. to my MS. copy of the K, and accord. to the TA: but in the CK this signification is omitted; for instead of والخَبْتُ وَبِالضَّمِّ السَّذَابُ, we find وَالخَفْتُ بِالفَتْحِ وَبِالضَّمِّ السَّذَابُ, meaning that حَفْتٌ and خُفْتٌ signify the same as سَذَابٌ.]) خُفَاتٌ [an inf. n. of 1, q. v. === And also used as an epithet; for ذُو خُفَاتٍ]: see خَفْتٌ. === Also Weak hearing. (TA, from a trad.) === لَسْتُ خُفَاتًا [I am not weak, and abject, or abased]. (T, from a verse of El-Jaadee.) خُفْتٌ [and accord. to the CK خَفْتٌ, but this is app. a mistake, (see what next precedes,)] i. q. سَذَابٌ [i. e. Rue]; (K;) as also خُتْفٌ. (T, TA.)
